2606.09630.title: ReCoVLA: VLM-Guided Reward Compilation for Failure Recovery in Vision-Language-Action Policies
2606.09630.paper_summary: Vision-language-action (VLA) policies provide strong priors for language-conditioned manipulation, but remain brittle in off-nominal states requiring targeted recovery. We propose ReCoVLA -- a failure-conditioned residual recovery framework that keeps a pretrained VLA policy frozen, uses an external vision-language model (VLM) to infer the failure mode and recovery stage, and compiles a structured reward from task-relevant components. Rather than using the VLM to generate actions or rewards directly, ReCoVLA uses it as a semantic reward selector: it predicts a recovery descriptor and reward mask for in-simulation residual-policy training, followed by zero-shot sim-to-real deployment of the trained recovery policies. This decouples high-level failure understanding from low-level corrective control to support different VLAs. Experiments across short-horizon, long-horizon, and contact-rich manipulation tasks show that ReCoVLA outperforms the tested baselines on average. In simulation, our reward compiler improves average success from 36.7% for the fine-tuned $π_{0.5}$ baseline to 66.7%. In physical zero-shot sim-to-real experiments, ReCoVLA achieves the best average performance, with 61.7% success.
2606.09630.paper_summary_zh: 視覺-語言-行動 (VLA) 策略為語言條件的操作提供了強有力的先驗，但在需要針對性恢復的非標準狀態下仍然脆弱。我們提出了 ReCoVLA -- 一種失效條件的殘差恢復框架，該框架保持預訓練的 VLA 策略不變，利用外部視覺-語言模型 (VLM) 來推斷失效模式和恢復階段，並從任務相關組件中編輯結構化獎勵。ReCoVLA 並不直接使用 VLM 生成行動或獎勵，而是將其用作語義獎勵選擇器：它預測恢復描述符和獎勵遮罩以進行模擬中的殘差策略訓練，隨後進行訓練後的零樣本模擬到現實部署。這將高層次的失效理解與低層次的修正控制解耦，以支持不同的 VLA。針對短期、長期和接觸豐富的操作任務的實驗顯示，ReCoVLA 在平均上超越了測試的基準。在模擬中，我們的獎勵編譯器將微調的 $π_{0.5}$ 基準的平均成功率從 36.7% 提高到 66.7%。在物理零樣本模擬到現實的實驗中，ReCoVLA 達到了最佳的平均表現，成功率為 61.7%。
2606.09630.author: Haodi Hu et.al.
2606.09630.authors: Haodi Hu, Chung-Ta Huang, Jing Liu, Ye Wang, Kei Suzuki, Matthew Brand, Toshiaki Koike-Akino
